scenario: Next.js SSR render path tracing and request ID threading

steps.step: Orient in the Next.js server architecture

map_quality.rationale: Names all key server classes with file:line (BaseServer at base-server.ts:316, NextNodeServer at next-server.ts:175, NextCustomServer at next.ts:365, DevServer, EdgeRouteModuleWrapper) and walks the three major subdirectories (app-render, route-modules, response-cache) with concrete file inventories. An agent could navigate straight to the right server class to extend.

specificity.rationale: Class names are exact (NextNodeServer, AppPageRouteModule, PagesRouteModule) and the Pages-vs-App split is made concrete with files and line numbers (render.tsx:457 vs app-render/app-render.tsx:3077, plus their route-module wrappers at module.ts:136 and :155).

justification.rationale: Subdirectories include short purpose annotations ("WorkStore ALS", "Server Actions", "Batcher+LRU") and the Pages/App split is shown via paired loader+renderer+route-module triples, but it doesn't explicitly explain WHY the class hierarchy exists (Edge vs Node, custom server seam, dev vs prod) — the reader has to infer it from class names.

uncertainty.rationale: Edge runtime is called out via EdgeRouteModuleWrapper and response-cache/web.ts, but the answer does not flag the dev-vs-prod server split (DevServer is listed but not flagged as a divergent code path) nor Turbopack-vs-webpack as configuration branches that can change which code path actually runs.

map_quality.rationale: Provides a complete pipeline walk from `BaseServer.handleRequest` (:877) → `handleRequestImpl` (:968) → `render`/`renderImpl` → `renderToResponse` → `findPageComponents`/`loadComponents` → `RouteModule.handleResponse` → Pages `renderToHTMLImpl` (render.tsx:457) and App `renderToHTMLOrFlight` (:3077) → `renderToHTMLOrFlightImpl` (:2568), each with file:line and the next method call.

specificity.rationale: Distinguishes outer `renderToHTMLOrFlight` (:3077) from inner `renderToHTMLOrFlightImpl` (:2568), names the public `renderToHTML` (:1622) vs `renderToHTMLImpl` (:457), and includes the outermost router entry (`handleRequest`/`handleRequestImpl`) rather than skipping to render.

justification.rationale: Explains the split via `routeModule kind` dispatch and the response-cache boundary (`handleResponse` → `responseGenerator` → `routeModule.render`), and shows what `loadComponents` returns (`{Component, App, Document, ComponentMod, routeModule}`) as the boundary handoff; could be slightly more explicit about WHY Pages vs App diverge.

uncertainty.rationale: Flags response-cache bypass concerns, RSC/flight streams, edge variant via `EdgeRouteModuleWrapper`, and error paths (`renderErrorImpl :2778`), but doesn't explicitly call out dynamic vs static prerender path divergence in the lifecycle walk itself.

map_quality.rationale: Lists the definition (app-render.tsx:3077), the direct caller (route-modules/app-page/module.ts:160 inside AppPageRouteModule.render), re-export sites (module.ts:9, :210; module.render.ts:9), and the indirect invocation path through BaseServer→responseGenerator. Agent could confidently change the signature with this map.

specificity.rationale: Callers cited with file:line and enclosing class/method ('AppPageRouteModule.render() method (class at :85)'), and the re-export point is explicitly named ('export { renderToHTMLOrFlight, vendored }' at module.ts:210). Subdirectory tags are present.

justification.rationale: Distinguishes definition vs direct call vs re-export vs lazy dynamic shim vs build-template re-export, explaining each role (e.g. 'lazy dynamic-require wrapper', 'responseGenerator → AppPageRouteModule.render'). Could be slightly more explicit about why module.compiled exists.

uncertainty.rationale: Flags the build-template re-export with 'verify by reading file when editing' and mentions module.compiled dynamic loading and edge wrapper. Doesn't explicitly call out edge-route-module-wrapper as a separate caller path or runtime route-module registration.

steps.step: Assess where to thread a request ID

map_quality.rationale: Names RequestMeta at request-meta.ts (~:308), handleRequestImpl (:968), RouteModule.handleResponse (:1102), ResponseCache.get (:445), edge wrapper (:34), and the affected tests — the agent has a complete edit map without needing to re-explore.

specificity.rationale: Cites concrete files, line numbers, and field locations (WorkStore field near :105, createWorkStore :3145, fromResponseCacheEntry/toResponseCacheEntry). No generic 'request metadata system' hand-waving; only a missing explicit NEXT_REQUEST_ID_HEADER constant name (likely doesn't exist yet, but not flagged).

justification.rationale: Explicitly contrasts RequestMeta vs ALS ('avoids a new ALS, already plumbed everywhere req lives') and explains cache-key tradeoff ('do NOT mix the request ID into it or every request becomes a MISS') — real reasoning, not a list.

uncertainty.rationale: Flags cache-HIT must emit current request's ID not producer's, edge runtime mirroring requirement, and renderOpts passthrough for pages userland. Misses an explicit callout of root Middleware as an interaction point.
